name: Barney Bigard
abstract: Albany Leon "Barney" Bigard (March 3, 1906 – June 27, 1980) was an American jazz clarinetist known for his 15-year tenure with Duke Ellington. He also played tenor saxophone.

concerts.label: Fred Robbins' One-Night Stand: Louis Armstrong

quotes.text: And every time I saw him he would want to grab me and hug me, you know. And then the last time -- the time that I was teed off on him was at the concert at Carnegie Hall when he was supposed to introduce some of his past musicians in his band. You know, for the Newport Jazz Festival.
quotes.caption: Bigard recalls Louis Armstrong's behavior at a Newport concert

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: It was just Barney Bigard playing in a very low register... But it sounded like two bass clarinets to me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Polites
about_quotes.evidence: I spoke to Barney Bigard when he was in Australia with Louis Armstrong in 1954

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: I later discovered that, what I heard there, I liked the music too. They were doing an Ellington medley. What they did there, that particular thing was Mood Indigo. They recorded this – I think a shorter version, where they do an Ellington medley. They did it so well that on a blindfold test Barney
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: I was pressed into service as a maraca player on one of the small band, Ellington band dates, under Barney Bigard's name... I played maracas a little bit later, in Barney Bigard's Orchestra.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Lesberg
about_quotes.evidence: Edmond Hall was on it with me when I went later on, and Barney Bigard and Velma Middleton. I think they fixed up about a six piece band and they went out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Lesberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Jim BEEBE on trombone, Barney Bigard, Barrett Deems on drums, Art Hodes on piano and me on cornet. And we're do that for three months...
